title: Boat Rental in Berlin Spree: A Budget Guide for 2026
html:
Exploring Berlin Spree by boat need not break the bank. With prices starting from €65 for a shared tour departing from Jannowitzbrücke, renting a boat in Berlin Spree is an affordable way to see the city from the water. BoatHire24 connects you with local owners offering everything from small motorboats to crewed yachts.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Berlin Spree?

Prices range from €65 for a shared one-hour tour to €850 for a full-day crewed yacht. Budget-friendly options include self-drive motorboats from €120 for two hours, while larger catamarans cost around €500 for a full day. For a premium experience, skip the driving with skippered charters in Berlin Spree, which include a captain and fuel from €350 for a half-day.

Where to set off in Berlin Spree

Several marinas and piers offer easy access to the Spree. Start at Jannowitzbrücke for central routes, or head to Marina Lübars for a quieter stretch. Treptow Pier near the park is ideal for family trips, while Charlottenburg Palace jetty gives a royal perspective. For longer cruises, set off from Spandau Harbour and venture towards the Havel River. Each location has boat hire services via BoatHire24.

Which boat to rent

Choose a boat that suits your group size and budget. Here are four popular types available through BoatHire24:

  1. Shared tour boat, Best for solo travellers or couples on a budget: up to 12 passengers per tour, from €65 per person.
  2. Small motorboat (e.g. Olympic 490), Ideal for a couple or small family exploring the city centre: 2-4 guests, from €120 for two hours.
  3. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 380), Perfect for a full-day group outing: up to 10 guests, from €500 for eight hours.
  4. Crewed motor yacht (e.g. Azimut 50), Best for luxury events or celebrations: up to 12 guests with skipper, from €850 for a full day.

For a mid-range option, see the Mefasa 90 charter page, which sleeps six and costs around €250 for a half-day self-drive.

Cost breakdown for boat hire in Berlin Spree

Understand what you pay at each price level. Below is a typical price band table (all prices in EUR):

  • Shared tour: €65 per person for a one-hour sightseeing trip, often with a guide.
  • Small motorboat half-day: €120, €200 for two hours, including basic safety equipment and fuel.
  • Catamaran full-day: €400, €600 for eight hours, ideal for groups of 8, 12.
  • Crewed yacht: €700, €1,200 for a full day, including skipper, fuel, and sometimes catering.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Berlin Spree?

Most small boats under 15 hp require no licence, but larger vessels may need a German Sportbootführerschein Binnen. Self-drive motorboats up to 15 hp are generally licence-free, though operators often require a brief safety briefing. For faster or longer boats, confirm requirements with the owner on BoatHire24. High season runs from May to September, when the Spree can be busy and the prevailing wind is from the west. A local tip:

Book a morning departure to beat the afternoon crowds and enjoy calmer water near the Museum Island.

At a glance

  • Price band: €65 (shared tour) to €1,200 (crewed yacht full-day)
  • Peak months: May, September
  • Trip length: 1 hour to 8 hours
  • Capacity: 1, 12 passengers per boat
  • Currency: EUR (all prices quoted)

Questions about boat rental in Berlin Spree

Can I rent a boat in Berlin Spree without a licence?

Yes, many small motorboats up to 15 hp are available without a licence; the operator will provide a short safety briefing before departure.

How far can I travel from the Spree to the Havel?

The distance from central Berlin to the Havel is about 10 nautical miles via the Spree and connecting waterways, a pleasant three-hour cruise.

What is the best time to rent a boat for sunset?

Sunset rentals are most popular from June to August, and it is wise to book at least a week in advance through a platform like BoatHire24.

Are there any hidden costs when hiring a boat?

Fuel is sometimes included, but always check the listing; some owners charge extra for cleaning or a security deposit.

Whether you are a first-time renter or a seasoned boater, finding a suitable vessel is straightforward via the BoatHire24 marketplace. If you own a boat in Berlin Spree, you can list it on BoatHire24 to reach thousands of potential renters. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Berlin Spree or list your own.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Berlin Spree and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
